priority_region.label: Central Europe
priority_region.notes: M20 second priority region. Habsburg / Bohemian Crown / Poland-Lithuania / Hungarian frontier readability for 1444. Outside these modern ISO parents, scaffold geometry is retained.

source_notes: Curator-authored soft frontier bands and sample hard overrides for M20 broader period geometry.
Not derived from proprietary game maps. Not a full OpenHistoricalMap extract.
Hard overrides target sample_* province IDs (sample_at_*, sample_cz_*, sample_pl_*, sample_hu_*); they skip cleanly on full Natural Earth builds.
Soft boundary hints are geographic and apply on any scaffold that intersects the priority region.
do_not_claim: Paradox-grade HRE / Bohemian Crown / Jagiellon microborders
legal 1444 frontiers — hints are illustrative for the historical sniff test

boundary_hints.label: Bohemian Crown core band
boundary_hints.notes: Soft band around Bohemia / Moravia readability where modern CZE outlines mislead for 1444.

boundary_hints.label: Ottoman–Hungarian frontier band
boundary_hints.notes: Illustrative southern Hungarian frontier after Varna (1444) — soft only.

boundary_hints.label: Habsburg–Hungarian corridor
boundary_hints.notes: Soft band between Vienna basin and Hungarian plain for dual-monarchy-before-it-was-dual readability.

boundary_hints.label: Polish–Lithuanian western approach
boundary_hints.notes: Illustrative western Polish / Mazovian band for Jagiellon-era framing.

boundary_hints.label: Silesian fringe band
boundary_hints.notes: Soft band for Bohemian Crown / Silesian story without a full redraw.

hard_overrides.reason: Separate Prague core from residual Bohemia so 1444 politics are not painted as modern CZE-wide admin.
Slight reshape toward a denser Hungarian plain footprint for 1444 readability.
Vienna basin retained as scaffold shape; Habsburg story via politics + corridor hint.
Mazovia retained; Jagiellon story via politics + western approach band.
